item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Item #: SCP-2479-A-5
Represented Animal: Domestic cat (F. catus)

whereas other Tertiary consumers such as SCP-2479-A-5 (considered representative of members of the species C. lupus)

Are SCP-2479-A-5 cats or wolves?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A neat little SCP! This one, in particular, caught my attention due to bits of it resembling (albeit only vaguely) a potential SCP I've been working on in my free time. The creation of -AX seems… a bit strange for the Foundation, as mentioned above. If the creation of -AX was due to regular experiments I'd understand, but the Foundation WANTED to create something like -AX, which is rather out-of-character for them.

However, despite my dislike for -AX near the end, I do like the rest of the article. +1 with the caveat that the -AX stuff gets some changes at some point in the future.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The basic premise is alright, but I absolutely don't understand why the Foundation would go full mad scientist and decide to "breed" an instance that's even more dangerous and difficult to contain. The bits about it being a superpredator to keep the population down don't make a lot of sense if the things were already preying on each other. And if even if they weren't, why can't the population be kept down by ordinary culling?

The result is that when the -AX escapes and mates with the female instances, the intended "oh crap" feeling is lost under a "what were they thinking!?"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i love abandoned amusement park SCPs, i love the elements of SCP-035 in this article, but the experimentation on, creation of, and subsequent use of -AX was a little bit too "supervillainy" for me. in any case, it's a pretty gripping entry, and the imagery is very evocative. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You know, it's been a while since I decided to revisit this, and I agree with all of you, novote tho since I like the concept enough to not downvote

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This went on at far too much length for my liking. The tropes (abandoned amusement park, masks that turn you into what they represent, weird animal hybrid is a killing/raping machine) aren't used to any great effect - there are no real surprises or character development to keep me engaged.

I'd suggest distilling this down to the core story idea you want to get across, and then reworking it to focus more clearly on that idea. Too much of this is unnecessary and killing the pacing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Just read it, it was great, and I am really excited for the testing log.

Oh sweet, testing log is out. I will definitely read this later.

item.title: Re: Staff Post: Open
item.content:encoded: Righteo! That brings your article into compliance with the images policy. THanks!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You could always use the term 'Avatism'

https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Atavism

item.title: Re: Staff Post: Open
item.content:encoded: Alright. I'll see if I can get permission, but until then I'll remove the images from the article.

item.title: Re: Staff Post: Open
item.content:encoded: Those images do not appear to be explicitly released under any particular license. As such it is assumed they would be under an exclusive copyright owned by the creator of the images. You can try to get permission directly from the people who made the things, but in the mean time they are still non-compliant.

item.title: Re: Staff Post: Open
item.content:encoded:
EDIT:

Image used with permission from Mimmi K.
Click here for source.

item.title: Staff Post: Open
item.content:encoded: Images used in posted articles must comply with the site's image policy. A source must be provided on the discussion page and the source must be compatible with the site's license. The author will be notified after this posting via Wikidot PM and will have 48 hours to properly source the image before it is edited out of the page. If the images are not wholly your own, please post the image's source as a link (or links) in reply to this post and edit it into your first post in this discussion thread.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Sorry about the tags. I reviewed the page of the collective tags in use across the site and couldn't find anything that suggested 'location' when I searched for it and its synonyms. Are you able to list which tags I should include?

EDIT: Just noticed the improved tags put there for me, thank you very much!

I have ideas for additional content in the future, such as testing logs which are currently being written, but right now I think the addendum is enough information to stir ideas in the reader's minds as to what could come of the experiments which the article implies are underway.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Please don't incompletely tag articles. The "immobile" tag is meant for anomalies that should be able to be moved, but cannot (like a bullet frozen in air); a location does not apply to that definition.

As for the article, I'm in agreement with Roget. There's a lot of untapped angles in regards to the masks themselves, the purpose of the park's ecosystem, etc. It felt predictable for an amusement park with horror tropes, which was dissappointing compared to some earlier articles.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
i scrolled down too fast and I scared myself
also well done +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I guess that's fine if you feel it's needed then.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it, personally. Although I do think that it might be better off in much shorter articles such as this one, it adds a professional tone to a document that I think is appropriate.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks for the advice.

I've moved the first quotation to the Special Containment Procedures section, thank you for pointing that out.

I thought about your first point and realised the contradictory nature of having a park filled with "mascots" that are violent towards guests for no apparent reason, and so have rewritten the third of the aggressive reaction criteria list. Thanks for the idea, and for instigating me to rewrite it: you were right, it was unnecessary.

"Primitive" was the second word I chose, after "animalistic".
I wanted to choose my words carefully to suggest that -A are bestial, but maintain basic human characteristics even after affliction. "Primitive" simply sounds more human, which I thought was in my best interests to preserve in an article with a strong human body horror element.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Two (2)"

You don't need to add the number next to the word, this actually disorients some readers, it's optional to remove them, but I recommend you do.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My personal theory falls in the vein of "Why you don't use 'Crazy Earl's Discount Thaumaturgy Depot' as your primary supplier of magical trinkets."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I dunno, I was all set for an anomalous amusement park, but this didn't really do anything for me. Maybe it's the nagging sense of wondering why someone would make this. I mean, your options when you enter are A) get attacked by animal people, or B) become one yourself, and rather quickly. Not exactly fun for the whole family. A couple notes:

Outside of testing, no personnel should enter SCP-2479's active zone at any time without permission from Site Director ██████.

This belongs in the containment procedures. I'd put it at the end of the first paragraph, myself.

a primitive scratching and biting manner

Tone. I'm not sure what makes this 'primitive' or differentiates it from other forms of the same act, to illustrate.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This had a gradually increasing body horror that worked for me. I have the sense that the transformed instances are functioning as elements of the anomaly; the loss of agency just adds to the squick.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks to both of you for the ideas!

I have since added a section to the article, marked EDIT as of 14/04/██. Hopefully it explores the content of the SCP a bit deeper and adds some potential for future developments.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's quite long for a relatively simple idea already.

I'd suggest that this one's a simple idea that gets more interesting and complex with additional exploration.

I'm an upvote myself as it stands, but I wouldn't mind seeing this expanded: that Elton idea has some twisted potential, methinks. Do want to know what happens if someone wears more than one mask, or an already transformed person wears a second (or third, or fourth, or fifth…) mask like Roget suggested, too- if nothing else its the sort of "poke it to see what it does" experiment the Foundation can't resist, and an excuse to go off on some weird tangents: Say, a hybrid ends up being uniquely well suited to the park's ecosystem and starts acting like an invasive species until they put it down, or finds an unfilled niche in the ecosystem.

You could also leave the number of locations ambiguous. Keep the list, just change the preceding sentence to not give an exact number, something like "major attractions include:". That would give you more room to play with minor attractions (say, a couple platypi associated with a fountain, that sort of thing) or for there to suddenly be a boat ride because you need a large pond for your crocodiles.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks a lot for the feedback.

I had other ideas for this article's expansion, but I omitted them because I feared that the end result would simply be too long. It's quite long for a relatively simple idea already.

I have a scrapped incident log I could revise and include. It was about the -As interacting with one another in ways that coincide with the Charles Elton food chain model. Do you think that would be more interesting material?

EDIT: Image sources, site image policy.

Image used with permission from Mimmi K.
Click here for source.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's not written poorly, but it never really grabbed my attention or had anything that really seemed interesting or unique for this article. Other amusement park articles have covered the ground here and I really feel not going more into the territory control of former amusement park creatures now trapped in this abandoned hellhole was a missed opportunity to give this SCP its own character. Perhaps if you expanded on that more, it could tie the mask effect into the main article better, since then you could say that they perhaps convert one another into different animal-like creatures, by repeatedly exposing each other to these masks, until they're all so mutated they can't even tell who should own what territory. I feel like there's still ground to be covered from this material. -1
